title: (EPIC) M4 scenario suite
description:
End-to-end scenarios:
- PR closes task with incomplete ACs → check pr fails
- PR adds finding without evidence → check pr fails
- PR mixes code + new findings → check pr fails (ADR-0009)
- Secret scanning catches simulated API key in record content
- JSON merge driver handles concurrent AC array edits without loss
- Force-push that rewrites .firetrail/records/ is detected (server-side via pre-receive scaffold and client-side via check pr)

See: docs/ROADMAP.md §M4.

title: (EPIC) ft-cli M4 — check pr + diff + lint + review + CI templates + pre-receive
description:
M4 CLI surface:
- firetrail check pr <base..head> [--strict] — runs the full ft-pr validation; exit code reflects pass/fail
- firetrail diff <base> <head> — show what changed in record terms
- firetrail diff --memory — only memory-record changes
- firetrail lint memory — static lint over current workspace
- firetrail review <id> — interactive review helper (lists state, AC progress, evidence)
- GitHub Actions workflow template in templates/firetrail.yml
- Pre-receive hook script (shell, calls firetrail check pr server-side)
- Configurable strictness via .firetrail/config.yml

See: docs/components/ft-cli.md M4 sections.

title: (EPIC) ft-pr — validation engine + merge driver
description:
The full firetrail check pr implementation:
- ADR-0009 enforcement: mixed code+memory commits flagged
- ADR-0010 enforcement: AC completeness for closed tasks
- ADR-0013 enforcement: evidence for high-stakes verified records
- ADR-0017 enforcement: state_hash chain integrity (via ft-history)
- Secret scanning: regex-based detection of API keys, tokens, credentials
- AC cap enforcement: per-record AC count limits
- Evidence resolution: fetch + validate evidence URLs
- Draft auto-expiry: flag drafts older than threshold
- Deprecated-reference detection: warn on references to Deprecated records
- 200 records < 60s (content-hash caching)

PLUS: JSON merge driver for three-way merge with array-order + AC-list special cases.

See: ADRs above, docs/ROADMAP.md §M4.

title: (EPIC) M4 — PR Safety + CI
description:
Parent epic for M4. Memory governance enforced rather than convention. firetrail check pr validates PRs against the full rule set (ADR-0009, ADR-0010, ADR-0013, ADR-0017). GitHub Action template ships. JSON merge driver lands. AC enforcement, evidence requirements, secret scanning, AC caps in CI.

Roadmap: docs/ROADMAP.md §M4.

title: ft-search must materialise per-record trust from record body so trust transitions affect search ranking
description: M3 trust-weighting acceptance criterion ('hybrid search returns expected results; trust weighting prefers verified over reviewed over draft') is not fully exercisable from the CLI today. ft-search/src/engine.rs::load_record_meta (line ~447) derives trust via default_trust_for_kind(kind), which returns Reviewed for work-tracking kinds and Draft for memory kinds — completely ignoring promotions persisted in the record body (Finding::trust et al). Consequence: a finding promoted draft -> reviewed -> verified still sorts as Draft in search results, and the --trust verified filter excludes all memory records regardless of their real state. Fix: read trust out of the record body (or persist it on the records table at index time) so promotions are visible to ranking. Affects: crates/ft-search/src/engine.rs (load_record_meta + the schema population that feeds records). Surfaced by: crates/ft-cli/tests/scenarios/m3-trust-weighting.yml (which currently asserts the kind-driven trust ordering as a documented workaround), crates/ft-cli/tests/scenarios/m3-search-lexical.yml (--trust verified asserts empty hits, also a workaround).

title: ft-cli memory create paths must call WorkCtx::save_record so search FTS stays in sync
description: M3 scenario authoring uncovered that finding/runbook/decision/incident/memory create handlers go via write_with_create -> write_with_history -> index.refresh, skipping the WorkCtx::save_record path that performs SearchEngine::upsert_lexical. As a result, freshly created memory records are NOT searchable via 'firetrail search' until 'firetrail index rebuild' is run. The M3 roadmap acceptance criterion 'searchable within 1s of write completion' currently requires this rebuild workaround. Fix: route memory_create paths through save_record (or extract upsert_search_lexical and call it from write_with_create). Affects: crates/ft-cli/src/commands/memory_create.rs. Surfaced by: crates/ft-cli/tests/scenarios/m3-search-lexical.yml (see 'rebuild index so search FTS rows materialise for memory kinds').

title: (EPIC) M3 scenario suite
description:
End-to-end scenarios for M3:
- Embedding round-trip: finding embedded asynchronously, searchable within 1s
- Hybrid search ranking: trust weighting prefers verified > reviewed > draft on fixture
- prime --task <id> respects budget; truncation populates omitted manifest
- Lexical fallback: with onnx feature off, search results carry mode=lexical
- Daemon survives multiple concurrent CLI processes

See: docs/ROADMAP.md §M3 success criteria.

title: (EPIC) ft-cli M3 — search, similar, prime, daemon
description:
M3 CLI surface:
- firetrail search <query> [--mode lexical|hybrid] [--trust] [--scope]
- firetrail similar <id> [--limit n]
- firetrail prime --task <id> | --query <q> [--max-tokens n] [--format markdown|json]
- firetrail index rebuild | refresh
- firetrail daemon start | stop | status
- firetrail doctor — embedding-related checks (cache integrity, daemon liveness)

See: docs/components/ft-cli.md M3 sections.

title: (EPIC) ft-embed — embedding daemon + cache
description:
Embedding architecture per ADR-0007:
- Single-daemon design with Unix-socket queue
- Content-hash cache (skip re-embedding identical bodies)
- Integrity checksums on cached embeddings
- Default model: bge-small-en-v1.5 via ort (feature-gated; lexical-only when 'onnx' feature off)
- Async embed-on-write: records become searchable within 1s of commit

Includes daemon lifecycle (start/stop/status), socket protocol, integrity verification.

See: ADR-0007 local embeddings daemon.

title: (EPIC) ft-prime — context pack generation
description:
Pure-text context pack generation. Deterministic prioritization (trust, recency, scope distance, relevance), token-budget accounting, omitted manifest. Markdown + JSON output formats.

Standalone — no ML. Consumes ft-core records, ft-storage for reads, ft-index for ranking inputs.

See: ADR-0019 (prime context budget), docs/ROADMAP.md §M3.

title: (EPIC) ft-search — lexical + vector hybrid search
description:
Search backend:
- Lexical: SQLite FTS5 over record bodies (always available)
- Vector: sqlite-vec extension when present (feature-gated; M3 fallback to lexical-only when vec unavailable)
- Hybrid ranking: combines similarity + recency + trust weighting + scope distance
- Returns ranked Vec<SearchHit> with mode marker (lexical|hybrid|vector)

See: docs/ROADMAP.md §M3 hybrid search criteria.

title: (EPIC) M3 — Search + Prime
description:
Parent epic for M3. The corpus becomes discoverable: vector embeddings (ONNX, bge-small) + lexical fallback (BM25/FTS5), hybrid ranking by similarity + recency + trust + scope. firetrail prime generates token-budgeted context packs. AI agents can self-prime.

Roadmap: docs/ROADMAP.md §M3.

title: (EPIC) M2 scenario suite
description:
End-to-end scenarios validating M2:
- Incident → finding workflow with memory-only PR
- Trust transition: finding cannot reach verified without second reviewer; agents cannot promote
- Force-push detection: firetrail verify catches broken prev_state_hash chain
- Branch salvage: 3 findings + 1 task deleted; salvage prompts correctly; memory-only PR opens for findings

See: docs/ROADMAP.md §M2 success criteria.

title: (EPIC) ft-cli M2 — memory commands + lifecycle + capture + verify
description:
Add M2 command surface:
- Create: incident create, finding create, runbook create, decision create, gotcha create, memory create
- Capture: firetrail capture — opportunistic memory capture from terminal context
- Verify: firetrail verify — walk history chain, detect gaps
- Memory lifecycle: review, promote, deprecate, archive, supersede, merge, redact

Trust transitions enforce reviewer/origin rules. Agents cannot promote to verified.

See: docs/components/ft-cli.md (M2 sections in spec).

title: (EPIC) ft-history — state-hash chain + verify + compaction
description:
Implement history chain logic: prev_state_hash links each version to its predecessor. firetrail verify walks the chain and detects gaps (force-push detection per ADR-0017). PR-time compaction reduces history bloat while preserving auditability.

History is stored alongside records; ft-storage extensions provide append/compact primitives.

See: ADR-0003, ADR-0017.
close_reason: Implemented in same commit as ft-trust (cb41bc3); see commit body and crates/ft-history/. append_history, verify_chain, verify_repository, compact_history all green. 275 workspace tests pass.

title: (EPIC) ft-storage M2 — memory-PR detection + history append/compact
description:
Extend ft-storage:
- Memory-only PR detection on commit (per ADR-0009): commits touching only memory records get a flag the CI can enforce
- History append: write a history entry alongside each record mutation
- History compact: reduce verbose history at PR time while preserving prev_state_hash chain

See: ADR-0009, ADR-0003.

title: (EPIC) ft-trust — trust state machine
description:
Implement the trust state machine: draft → reviewed → verified. Evidence requirements per state (verified requires N reviewers, evidence). origin flag (human|agent|hybrid). Risk-class taxonomy.

Agents cannot promote to verified. Trust transitions are auditable (state_hash chain).

See: ADR-0013 trust model.

title: (EPIC) ft-core M2 — memory record types
description:
Extend ft-core with M2 record types: Incident, Finding, Runbook, Decision, Gotcha, Memory. Each has body schema, serde, JSON-schema generation, and integrates with the existing Record envelope.

Common fields: severity (incident/finding), risk_class, evidence collection, related_incident/related_finding links, deprecation status.

See: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md memory model, ADR-0013 trust model.

title: (EPIC) M2 — Incident Memory
description:
Parent epic for M2. Team can record findings, runbooks, decisions, gotchas, incidents with trust states. History chain (state_hash/prev_state_hash) is functional. Branch salvage catches abandoned-branch loss. Memory-only PRs enforced for findings/decisions/incidents/runbooks.

Roadmap: docs/ROADMAP.md §M2.

title: M1 scenario suite
description:
End-to-end scenarios that validate the M1 promise.

**Deliverables:**
- tests/scenarios/m1-happy-path.scenario — init → epic → tasks with deps → ACs
  added → tasks claimed and closed in dependency order → board reflects state
- tests/scenarios/m1-ac-enforcement.scenario — close fails with incomplete ACs,
  succeeds after ACs checked, force-close with reason works
- tests/scenarios/m1-conflict-resolution.scenario — two branches add tasks under
  the same epic; merge via JSON merge driver succeeds; both tasks present on main
- tests/scenarios/m1-index-rebuild.scenario — delete index.db; next command
  rebuilds cleanly; queries return same results as before deletion
- tests/scenarios/m1-clean-clone.scenario — clone the repo; first firetrail
  command works without manual setup

**Acceptance:**
- All M1 scenarios pass
- Total scenario suite runs in under 30 seconds (per ROADMAP §M1 success criteria)
- Each scenario has clear failure messages when assertions fail
- Verifier subagent has independently confirmed the scenarios match the M1
  success-criteria list from docs/ROADMAP.md

title: ft-cli work-graph commands
description:
The full M1 command surface for managing work.

**Crate:** ft-cli (extends E-M1-08)

**Commands:**
- Create: epic create, task create, subtask create, bug create
- Update: update <id> --status|--owner|--priority|--title
- Close: close <id> (validates ACs; --force --reason to override)
- Claim: claim <id>, unclaim <id> (mandatory claim_expires_at per ADR-0008)
- Link: link <from> <to> --type <kind>, dep add <from> <to> --type <kind>,
  dep remove <from> <to>
- Show: show <id> — full record with relations and history
- List: list [--type <t>] [--status <s>] [--owner <o>]
- Acceptance criteria: criteria add <id> "<text>", criteria list <id>,
  criteria check <id> <n>, criteria uncheck <id> <n>,
  criteria evidence <id> <n> --url <url>
- Ready: ready — list unblocked work
- Views: board, graph <id> (ASCII tree)

**Acceptance:**
- Every command has integration tests against TestRepo
- close refuses when ACs incomplete; --force --reason works
- claim is atomic: two concurrent claims on the same record — one wins, one fails
  with a clear error
- board and graph produce stable, snapshottable output (insta tests)
- --json output is documented and stable

title: ft-cli scaffold + init + doctor
description:
Clap entry point, command dispatch, and the two bootstrap commands.

**Crate:** ft-cli

**Commands shipped in this epic:**
- firetrail init — creates .firetrail/ structure, writes default config, installs
  git hooks (post-checkout, post-merge, pre-commit), writes .gitignore additions,
  optionally creates AGENTS.md / .claude/skills/firetrail/
- firetrail doctor — verifies config, storage, index, git status, schema version,
  reports issues with actionable fix hints

**Global options:**
- --format markdown|json (default: markdown for ttys, json for non-ttys)
- --quiet
- --verbose

**Acceptance:**
- firetrail init in an empty git repo produces a working .firetrail/ that
  subsequent commands can use without further setup
- firetrail doctor on a fresh init reports clean
- firetrail doctor after a manual corruption (delete index.db) reports the issue
  and suggests firetrail index rebuild
- All commands accept --json and produce machine-readable output
- Help text follows clap conventions; firetrail help <cmd> works

title: ft-identity basic resolution
description:
Implement basic identity resolution for M1. Full registry features are M5 (ADR-0008).

**Crate:** ft-identity

**Public API:**
- Identity re-exported from ft-core
- resolve_identity() returns the current actor, checking in order:
  1. FIRETRAIL_AUTHOR env var
  2. .firetrail/config.yml identity.name
  3. git config user.email
- Strict-mode flag in config: if true, fail loudly when no identity resolvable
- IdentityError type

**M5 deferred:** identity registry, aliases, capabilities, kinds, on-behalf-of,
offboarding sweep. M1 ships the resolution path only.

**Acceptance:**
- Resolution from each source works and is tested
- Strict mode rejects unknown identities
- Resolution failure produces a clear error message naming the order checked
- Integration test: TestRepo with various env var states resolves correctly

title: ft-index SQLite schema and basic queries
description:
Build the SQLite-backed read index. Vector search (sqlite-vec) is M3.

**Crate:** ft-index

**Public API:**
- Index::open(path) — open or create the index database at .firetrail/index.db
- Index::rebuild_from(storage) — full rebuild from the storage layer
- Index::refresh_incremental(storage, changed_paths) — diff-driven update
- Queries:
  - list_by(filter) with filters for type, status, owner, scope (scope is M5; for
    M1 just plumb the field through)
  - ready() — records with no unresolved blockers, not closed, not claimed
  - dependency_walk(id, direction) — walk blocks/blocked-by graph
  - show(id) — full record with relations
- IndexError

**SQLite schema (M1 subset):**
- records(id, type, title, status, priority, owner, created_by, created_at, ...)
- labels(record_id, key, value)
- relations(from_id, to_id, kind, created_at, created_by)
- acceptance_criteria(id, record_id, text, status, evidence_url, checked_by, ...)
- evidence(id, record_id, evidence_type, url, description, created_at, created_by)

**Acceptance:**
- Rebuild from a fixture corpus produces identical query results to direct file reads
- Incremental refresh handles add/modify/delete deltas correctly
- Ready detection respects blocked-by relations
- Performance: list-1000-records under 100ms; ready under 200ms
- Property tests on dependency walk

title: ft-storage embedded mode JSON read/write
description:
Implement the storage layer for embedded mode (ADR-0006). External mode is M5.

**Crate:** ft-storage

**Public API:**
- Storage trait: read_record(id), write_record(record), list_records(filter),
  delete_record(id), read_record_at_ref(ref, id)
- EmbeddedStorage implementation — operates against
  .firetrail/records/<type>/<id>.json
- Atomic write: write to <id>.json.tmp, rename to <id>.json
- Path conventions consistent with ADR-0002 and ADR-0015 (lowercase IDs)
- StorageError type

**Acceptance:**
- Round-trip: write a record, read it back, fields identical
- Concurrent writes from two threads do not corrupt records (atomic rename)
- List records by type returns expected set; pagination works
- Reading a record at a non-existent ref returns NotFound, not garbage
- Property tests for path encoding (handles unicode, special chars)
- Integration tests use TestRepo

title: ft-git git operations wrapper
description:
Wrap git operations behind a clean Rust API used by ft-storage and ft-cli.

**Crate:** ft-git

**Public API:**
- Repo::open(path), Repo::status(), Repo::head(), Repo::current_branch()
- Repo::list_files(ref, glob) — list files matching glob at a given ref
- Repo::read_file_at_ref(ref, path) — read file contents at a ref
- Repo::diff(from_ref, to_ref, path_filter)
- Repo::is_clean(), Repo::has_uncommitted(path)
- Repo::branches(), Repo::branch_create(name, from), Repo::branch_delete(name)
- Repo::install_hook(name, content) — install a post-checkout/post-merge hook
- Error types: GitError (thiserror)

**Implementation:** prefer gix for reads (modern, pure-Rust). Shell out to git
for operations that gix does not yet cover (e.g., complex merge operations later).

**Acceptance:**
- TestRepo from ft-testkit uses ft-git under the hood
- Read a file from main branch while checked out on a feature branch works
- All public methods have doc tests
- Property tests for path-glob filtering

title: ft-core record types and schema
description:
Implement the canonical record types for M1: Task, Subtask, Bug, Epic,
AcceptanceCriterion, Evidence. Plus shared fields and types.

**Crate:** ft-core

**Public API:**
- Record enum: Record::Task(Task), Record::Epic(Epic), etc.
- Per-type structs with strongly-typed fields (status, priority, owner are enums)
- RecordId newtype wrapper around String with hash-prefix display logic (ADR-0015)
- Identity newtype (ADR-0008, basic form)
- state_hash and prev_state_hash fields (computation logic is ft-history; ft-core
  only declares them)
- Acceptance criterion type with status, evidence_url, checked_by, checked_at
- Evidence type with evidence_type enum
- Serde implementations for canonical JSON serialization
- JSON Schema validation via jsonschema or hand-rolled validator
- Dependency relation enum (blocks, blocked-by, parent-of, etc. from FR-020)

**Acceptance:**
- All record types round-trip through serde_json
- Property tests (proptest) for record roundtrip on arbitrary inputs
- Schema validation rejects malformed records
- Hash computation is deterministic and excludes state_hash and prev_state_hash
  from its own input
- Display of RecordId produces unambiguous prefixes given a candidate set

title: ft-testkit foundation
description:
Build the test infrastructure that all other M1 epics consume.

**Crate:** ft-testkit

**Public API:**
- TestRepo::new() returns an isolated tempdir + initialized git repo + initialized
  .firetrail/ directory
- TestRepo::commit(msg), TestRepo::branch(name), TestRepo::checkout(name)
- Record factories: make_task(), make_epic(), make_finding(), etc. with builder-style
  overrides
- Assertion helpers: assert_record_exists(id), assert_field(id, field, value),
  assert_dep(from, to, kind)
- Scenario runner skeleton — parses YAML/RON scenario files, executes commands
  against a TestRepo, asserts expected state

**Acceptance:**
- A trivial scenario file ("create a task, assert it exists") runs and passes
- TestRepo cleanup is automatic (Drop impl removes tempdir)
- Factories produce records that round-trip cleanly through serde_json
- ft-testkit is a dependency of ft-core's test target (sanity check)

**Note:** Scenario runner only needs the skeleton at this stage. Full scenario
coverage lives in E-M1-10. Other M1 epics use TestRepo and factories.

title: Workspace skeleton + tooling
description:
Set up the Cargo workspace, crate scaffolding, CI workflow, and developer tooling.

**Crates created (empty skeletons):**
ft-core, ft-storage, ft-index, ft-embed, ft-identity, ft-scope, ft-trust,
ft-history, ft-search, ft-prime, ft-pr, ft-import, ft-git, ft-cli, ft-testkit.

**Deliverables:**
- Workspace `Cargo.toml` with shared dependencies declared via `[workspace.dependencies]`
- Each crate has its own `Cargo.toml` with placeholder lib.rs / main.rs
- `rustfmt.toml`, `.clippy.toml`, `rust-toolchain.toml` (stable, 2024 edition)
- `scripts/validate.sh` running fmt + clippy + nextest
- Pre-commit hook installer (writes to `.git/hooks/pre-commit`)
- GitHub Actions workflow running the full validation gate
- `justfile` (or `Makefile`) with common commands

**Acceptance:**
- `cargo build --workspace` succeeds with empty crates
- `cargo nextest run --workspace` runs (zero tests is OK at this stage)
- `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- `./scripts/validate.sh` exits 0
- CI workflow file exists and is syntactically valid

title: M1 — Local Work Graph
description:
Milestone: ship a usable local work-graph CLI.

A single engineer can run `firetrail init`, create epics and tasks, declare
dependencies, add acceptance criteria, claim work, and close work. Board view
shows state. Graph view shows blockers. JSON-in-Git storage with rebuildable
SQLite read index. Embedded mode only.

See docs/ROADMAP.md §M1 for the full scope and gates.

**Shippable promise.** Replace lightweight personal task tracking on a single repo.
Not yet competitive with full team tools (no memory, no search, no PR enforcement).

**Gates.**
- 100% of M1 acceptance criteria covered by tests
- Verifier subagent has signed off on each component PR
- `firetrail doctor` reports clean repo after the scripted scenario
- Single-binary distribution for macOS (arm64, x86_64) and Linux (x86_64)

title: M1 scenario suite
description:
End-to-end scenarios that validate the M1 promise.

**Deliverables:**
- `tests/scenarios/m1-happy-path.scenario` — init → epic → tasks with deps → ACs added
  → tasks claimed and closed in dependency order → board reflects state at each step
- `tests/scenarios/m1-ac-enforcement.scenario` — close fails with incomplete ACs,
  succeeds after ACs checked, force-close with reason works
- `tests/scenarios/m1-conflict-resolution.scenario` — two branches add tasks under
  the same epic; merge via JSON merge driver succeeds; both tasks present on main
- `tests/scenarios/m1-index-rebuild.scenario` — delete index.db; next command rebuilds
  cleanly; queries return same results as before deletion
- `tests/scenarios/m1-clean-clone.scenario` — clone the repo; first firetrail command
  works without manual setup

**Acceptance:**
- All M1 scenarios pass
- Total scenario suite runs in under 30 seconds (per ROADMAP §M1 success criteria)
- Each scenario has clear failure messages when assertions fail
- Verifier subagent has independently confirmed the scenarios match the M1
  success-criteria list from docs/ROADMAP.md
close_reason: Orphans from earlier failed script run; superseded by correctly wired set

title: ft-cli work-graph commands
description:
The full M1 command surface for managing work.

**Crate:** ft-cli (extends E-M1-08)

**Commands:**
- Create: `epic create`, `task create`, `subtask create`, `bug create`
- Update: `update <id> --status|--owner|--priority|--title`
- Close: `close <id>` (validates ACs; `--force --reason` to override)
- Claim: `claim <id>`, `unclaim <id>` (mandatory `claim_expires_at` per ADR-0008)
- Link: `link <from> <to> --type <kind>`, `dep add <from> <to> --type <kind>`,
  `dep remove <from> <to>`
- Show: `show <id>` — full record with relations and history
- List: `list [--type <t>] [--status <s>] [--owner <o>]`
- Acceptance criteria: `criteria add <id> "<text>"`, `criteria list <id>`,
  `criteria check <id> <n>`, `criteria uncheck <id> <n>`,
  `criteria evidence <id> <n> --url <url>`
- Ready: `ready` — list unblocked work
- Views: `board`, `graph <id>` (ASCII tree)

**Acceptance:**
- Every command has integration tests against TestRepo

- `claim` is atomic: two concurrent claims on the same record — one wins, one fails
  with a clear error
- `board` and `graph` produce stable, snapshottable output (insta tests)
- `--json` output is documented and stable

Clap entry point, command dispatch, and the two bootstrap commands.

**Crate:** ft-cli

**Commands shipped in this epic:**
- `firetrail init` — creates .firetrail/ structure, writes default config, installs
  git hooks (post-checkout, post-merge, pre-commit), writes .gitignore additions,
  optionally creates AGENTS.md / .claude/skills/firetrail/
- `firetrail doctor` — verifies config, storage, index, git status, schema version,
  reports issues with actionable fix hints

**Global options:**
- `--format markdown|json` (default: markdown for ttys, json for non-ttys)
- `--quiet`
- `--verbose`

**Acceptance:**

  commands can use without further setup
- `firetrail doctor` on a fresh init reports clean
- `firetrail doctor` after a manual corruption (delete index.db) reports the issue and
  suggests `firetrail index rebuild`
- All commands accept `--json` and produce machine-readable output
- Help text follows clap conventions; `firetrail help <cmd>` works
close_reason: Orphans from earlier failed script run; superseded by correctly wired set

title: ft-index SQLite schema and basic queries
description:
Build the SQLite-backed read index. Vector search (sqlite-vec) is M3.

**Crate:** ft-index

**Public API:**
- `Index::open(path)` — open or create the index database at `.firetrail/index.db`
- `Index::rebuild_from(storage)` — full rebuild from the storage layer
- `Index::refresh_incremental(storage, changed_paths)` — diff-driven update
- Queries:
  - `list_by(filter)` with filters for type, status, owner, scope (scope is M5; for
    M1 just plumb the field through)
  - `ready()` — records with no unresolved blockers, not closed, not claimed
  - `dependency_walk(id, direction)` — walk blocks/blocked-by graph
  - `show(id)` — full record with relations
- `IndexError`

**SQLite schema** (M1 subset):
- records(id, type, title, status, priority, owner, created_by, created_at, ...)
- labels(record_id, key, value)
- relations(from_id, to_id, kind, created_at, created_by)
- acceptance_criteria(id, record_id, text, status, evidence_url, checked_by, checked_at, ...)
- evidence(id, record_id, evidence_type, url, description, created_at, created_by)

**Acceptance:**
- Rebuild from a fixture corpus produces identical query results to direct file reads
- Incremental refresh handles add/modify/delete deltas correctly
- Ready detection respects blocked-by relations
- Performance: list-1000-records under 100ms; ready under 200ms
- Property tests on dependency walk

title: ft-identity basic resolution
description:
Implement basic identity resolution for M1. Full registry features are M5 (ADR-0008).

**Crate:** ft-identity

**Public API:**
- `Identity` re-exported from ft-core
- `resolve_identity()` returns the current actor, checking in order:
  1. FIRETRAIL_AUTHOR env var
  2. .firetrail/config.yml `identity.name`
  3. git config user.email
- Strict-mode flag in config: if true, fail loudly when no identity resolvable
- `IdentityError` type

**M5 deferred:** identity registry, aliases, capabilities, kinds, on-behalf-of,
offboarding sweep. M1 ships the resolution path only.

**Acceptance:**
- Resolution from each source works and is tested
- Strict mode rejects unknown identities
- Resolution failure produces a clear error message naming the order checked
- Integration test: TestRepo with various env var states resolves correctly
close_reason: Orphans from earlier failed script run; superseded by correctly wired set

title: ft-storage embedded mode JSON read/write
description:
Implement the storage layer for embedded mode (ADR-0006). External mode is M5.

**Crate:** ft-storage

**Public API:**
- `Storage` trait: `read_record(id)`, `write_record(record)`, `list_records(filter)`,
  `delete_record(id)`, `read_record_at_ref(ref, id)`
- `EmbeddedStorage` implementation — operates against `.firetrail/records/<type>/<id>.json`
- Atomic write: write to `<id>.json.tmp`, rename to `<id>.json`
- Path conventions consistent with ADR-0002 and ADR-0015 (lowercase IDs)
- StorageError type

**Acceptance:**
- Round-trip: write a record, read it back, fields identical
- Concurrent writes from two threads do not corrupt records (atomic rename)
- List records by type returns expected set; pagination works
- Reading a record at a non-existent ref returns NotFound, not garbage
- Property tests for path encoding (handles unicode, special chars)
- Integration tests use TestRepo
close_reason: Orphans from earlier failed script run; superseded by correctly wired set

title: ft-testkit foundation
description:
Build the test infrastructure that all other M1 epics consume.

**Crate:** ft-testkit

**Public API:**
- `TestRepo::new()` returns an isolated tempdir + initialized git repo + initialized
  .firetrail/ directory
- `TestRepo::commit(msg)`, `TestRepo::branch(name)`, `TestRepo::checkout(name)`
- Record factories: `make_task()`, `make_epic()`, `make_finding()`, etc. with
  builder-style overrides
- Assertion helpers: `assert_record_exists(id)`, `assert_field(id, field, value)`,
  `assert_dep(from, to, kind)`
- Scenario runner skeleton — parses YAML/RON scenario files, executes commands
  against a TestRepo, asserts expected state

**Acceptance:**
- A trivial scenario file ("create a task, assert it exists") runs and passes
- TestRepo cleanup is automatic (Drop impl removes tempdir)
- Factories produce records that round-trip cleanly through serde_json
- ft-testkit is a dependency of ft-core's test target (sanity check)

**Note:** Scenario runner only needs the skeleton at this stage. Full scenario
coverage lives in E-M1-10. Other M1 epics use TestRepo and factories.
close_reason: Orphans from earlier failed script run; superseded by correctly wired set

title: Workspace skeleton + tooling
description:
Set up the Cargo workspace, crate scaffolding, CI workflow, and developer tooling.

**Crates created (empty skeletons):**
ft-core, ft-storage, ft-index, ft-embed, ft-identity, ft-scope, ft-trust,
ft-history, ft-search, ft-prime, ft-pr, ft-import, ft-git, ft-cli, ft-testkit.

**Deliverables:**
- Workspace `Cargo.toml` with shared dependencies declared via `[workspace.dependencies]`
- Each crate has its own `Cargo.toml` with placeholder lib.rs / main.rs
- `rustfmt.toml`, `.clippy.toml`, `rust-toolchain.toml` (stable, 2024 edition)
- `scripts/validate.sh` running fmt + clippy + nextest
- Pre-commit hook installer (writes to `.git/hooks/pre-commit`)
- GitHub Actions workflow running the full validation gate
- `justfile` (or `Makefile`) with common commands

**Acceptance:**
- `cargo build --workspace` succeeds with empty crates
- `cargo nextest run --workspace` runs (zero tests is OK at this stage)
- `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- `./scripts/validate.sh` exits 0
- CI workflow file exists and is syntactically valid
close_reason: Orphans from earlier failed script run; superseded by correctly wired set

title: M1 — Local Work Graph
description:
Milestone: ship a usable local work-graph CLI.

A single engineer can run `firetrail init`, create epics and tasks, declare
dependencies, add acceptance criteria, claim work, and close work. Board view
shows state. Graph view shows blockers. JSON-in-Git storage with rebuildable
SQLite read index. Embedded mode only.

See docs/ROADMAP.md §M1 for the full scope and gates.

**Shippable promise.** Replace lightweight personal task tracking on a single repo.
Not yet competitive with full team tools (no memory, no search, no PR enforcement).

**Gates.**
- 100% of M1 acceptance criteria covered by tests
- Verifier subagent has signed off on each component PR
- `firetrail doctor` reports clean repo after the scripted scenario
- Single-binary distribution for macOS (arm64, x86_64) and Linux (x86_64)
close_reason: Orphans from earlier failed script run; superseded by correctly wired set

close_reason: duplicate of firetrail-6gj from failed first run

title: ft-history: relocate verify_repository to ft-storage permanently
description: M2 cycle-break: removed verify_repository / VerifyReport / RecordVerifyFailure from ft-history to drop its runtime dep on ft-storage so ft-storage could in turn depend on ft-history. The repository-wide verify walker is currently provided only via validate_pre_commit (per-path). A dedicated verify_repository in ft-storage (taking &EmbeddedStorage and Option<&Repo>, including the git-log force-push cross-check from ADR-0017) is still wanted for the firetrail verify CLI surface. See firetrail-pb3.

title: (EPIC) Branch salvage workflow
description:
Implement salvage workflow per ADR-0018:
- post-checkout / post-merge hooks installed by firetrail init
- firetrail memory salvage — interactive prompt for memory records on abandoned branches (default Y for findings, N for tasks)
- Memory-only PR auto-opens for salvaged records

See: ADR-0018.

title: firetrail list lazily rebuilds index when index.db is missing
description: Per docs/components/m1-scenarios.md §m1-index-rebuild and §m1-clean-clone, the first firetrail command after a clone (or after index.db deletion) should rebuild the index on its own — users should not need to run firetrail doctor --fix manually. Currently, the scenarios use doctor --fix as the recovery path. After the lazy-rebuild lands, simplify m1-index-rebuild.yml and m1-clean-clone.yml to call firetrail list directly. Parent epic: firetrail-939.

title: M1 ready query: read .firetrail/relations.jsonl for blocked-by edges
description: Today the ft-index 'ready' query checks the SQLite relations table, which is populated from inline body fields only — it does NOT mirror .firetrail/relations.jsonl (the interim store written by firetrail dep add). As a result, dep add A B --type blocked-by has no effect on ready ordering. The m1-happy-path scenario works around this by also setting B's status to blocked. Fix: either (a) the dep_add command also writes the relation into the SQLite relations table via ctx.index, or (b) the index refresh path reads relations.jsonl. Once fixed, m1-happy-path.yml can drop the simulate-blocked-status step. Parent epic: firetrail-939.

title: M1 conflict scenario: tighten to require JSON merge driver after child_ids mutation lands
description: The m1-conflict-resolution scenario at crates/ft-cli/tests/scenarios/m1-conflict-resolution.yml currently passes trivially because task create --epic does not mutate the epic's child_ids array (firetrail-1xc deferred that). Once child_ids mutation lands (M4 with ft-pr JSON merge driver), tighten the scenario to: (a) require the merge driver to be installed, (b) assert the epic's child_ids contains both task IDs in sorted order after the merge, (c) assert no <<< conflict markers in any record file. Parent epic: firetrail-939.

title: task
description:
firetrail-1xc writes link/dep relations to .firetrail/relations.jsonl (interim per ADR/firetrail-tq7). show/graph read this file directly. However, ft-index ready/graph queries that follow blocked-by edges only see the SQL relations table, which today is only populated from inline body fields (parent_epic, parent_task, child_ids).

So 'firetrail dep add A B --type blocked-by' currently has NO effect on 'firetrail ready' filtering — A will still appear ready even though it has a blocker. firetrail-tq7 fixes this by extending ft-index rebuild_from to consume an explicit Relation graph.

In the meantime, agents using 'ready' should be aware that blocks added via 'dep add' (not parent-of/child-of derived from body) are advisory only.

title: Upgrade gix to drop RUSTSEC-2025-0140 / RUSTSEC-2025-0021 advisories
description: deny.toml ignores two transitive gix advisories (gix-path Non-utf8 String, sha1-collisiondetection). Revisit when gix upstream ships fixes and before PR import / untrusted commit ingestion lands (E-M1-10).

title: ft-index relations from explicit graph (currently inline-only)
description: ft-index derives parent-of / child-of edges from inline body fields (Task.parent_epic, Subtask.parent_task, Epic.child_ids). blocks / blocked-by relations are NOT writable from inline fields — tests inject directly into the SQL relations table. When ft-storage exposes a Relation graph, extend rebuild_from to consume it and drop the test-only side-channel.

title: Test
description: test

title: ft-storage: compact_changed_in_pr should report skipped paths
description: compact_changed_in_pr silently skips Deleted entries, Config/Other paths, and records whose file is missing in the working tree. CI surfaces would benefit from a richer return type (e.g. CompactRunReport with reasons) so the operator can audit what was and wasn't touched.

title: ft-storage: deleted-record handling in validate_pre_commit
description: validate_pre_commit currently uses storage.read(id) which returns NotFound for deletions. A delete inside a PR is legitimate (e.g. redaction) but the current report flags it as a failure. Wire in a 'deletion intent' signal (likely from ChangeKind::Deleted in the diff) so deletions are surfaced as a distinct PathReport class rather than a hash failure.

title: ft-identity: parse full ADR-0008 identities: registry block
description: .firetrail/identity.yml parses only name and email at M1; the full identities: registry block is silently ignored (forward-compatible). M5 should extend the deserializer.

title: ft-identity: wire StatusReport via ft-git (currently shells out)
description: ft-identity shells out to git config --get user.email/name directly to avoid coupling with the in-flux ft-git crate. Now that ft-git is stable, refactor to consume ft_git::StatusReport or equivalent. Touch point: crates/ft-identity/src/lib.rs git_config source.

title: ft-index: populate last_indexed_commit from ft-git::head()
description: Schema column exists; only last_rebuild_at is populated today. Wire ft_git::head() once stable so incremental refresh can short-circuit on no-op commits.

title: Replace ft-index local Storage shim with ft_storage::Storage
description: ft-index defines a local Storage trait + StorageError + StorageFilter in crates/ft-index/src/storage.rs as an interim because ft-storage is a stub. When ft-storage lands its canonical Storage trait, delete the shim and re-export from there. Also update IndexError::Storage(#[from] StorageError) to wrap the real type.

title: Harmonize ft-testkit record/relation paths with ft-storage
description: ft-testkit defines RECORDS_DIR + record_path() inline and stores relations as flat .firetrail/relations.json — interim until ft-storage owns the layout. When ft-storage lands, replace inline writes with delegation. Search crates/ft-testkit/src/paths.rs and assertions.rs for call sites.

title: Replace ft-testkit testkit: virtual commands with real firetrail binary
description: ft-testkit scenario runner has a testkit:create-task / testkit:create-epic / testkit:assert-exists dispatch as a stand-in because ft-cli is a stub. Once firetrail init/task create/etc. exist, port scenarios to call the real binary and remove (or feature-gate) the testkit: shortcuts. Touch points: crates/ft-testkit/src/scenario.rs and tests/scenarios/.

title: ft-testkit cross-crate sanity check from ft-core tests
description: ft-testkit spec acceptance #7 says ft-testkit must be consumed by ft-core's integration tests as a sanity check. Currently scoped only to ft-testkit's own tests. Add ft-testkit as a dev-dep to ft-core and write a test that uses TestRepo to round-trip a ft-core Record.

title: Prefix-based RecordId resolution from candidate set
description: RecordId::from_string requires full 64-char hex tail. CLI/storage layers need prefix-based resolution (ADR-0015) — accept a short prefix, return the unique RecordId from a candidate set or error on ambiguity. ft-core has unambiguous_display building block.

title: Export ft-core JSON schema to docs/schema/
description: ft-core::record_schema_json() exposes the schema in-memory. Wire a small script or ft-cli subcommand to write it to docs/schema/firetrail-record-v1.json so external consumers can pin against it. Mentioned as a follow-up in ft-core (firetrail-kss) acceptance review.

title: ft-storage: partial-parse optimization for list()
description: EmbeddedStorage::list / iter full-parses + schema-validates every file. Production uses ft-index so this is fine, but a partial-parse path (envelope only for filter, full only for matching records) helps large repos when ft-index is unavailable.

title: ft-storage: crash-injection atomicity test
description: Spec acceptance #2 calls for atomicity verification via fault injection (panic between sync_all and rename; assert no half-written file remains). Current parallel-race tests give equivalent confidence but not the spec-mandated form. Best after ft-cli lands so we can fork-and-kill a real binary.

title: ft-identity: implement strict-mode rejection via M5 registry
description: Strict mode is stored on the resolver and surfaced in ResolutionTrace, but doesn't reject. Spec acceptance #7 expects M5 to wire this to the registry. File for M5.

title: ft-index: performance gate behind --features slow-tests
description: Spec calls for a 1,000-record fixture perf test. Deferred at M1. Add behind feature flag so CI doesn't pay the cost by default.

title: Wire install_hooks config in ft-testkit TestRepoConfig
description: TestRepoConfig.install_hooks is currently a no-op. Wire it once ft-cli init --hooks (or equivalent) exists so scenarios can exercise hook behavior.

title: Nightly stress test for RecordId::mint uniqueness at scale
description: ADR-0015 acceptance implies 16M+ unique IDs should not collide. ft-core unit/proptest coverage caps at ~10K / 500 cases for CI speed. Add a #[ignore]'d test runnable in nightly CI to validate the literal acceptance bullet.

value: ft-git (firetrail-vth) implementation: uses gix 0.66 with revision+blob-diff features for reads (head/current_branch/is_detached/branches/branch_exists/read_file_at_ref/list_files_at_ref/log_path/diff); shells out to git for status/is_clean/has_uncommitted/branch_create/branch_delete/checkout; direct fs for install_hook/hook_installed/remove_hook with managed-region markers. Adds globset 0.4 as direct dep (not workspace). Repo struct only stores root PathBuf — opens fresh gix::Repository per call to avoid lifetime leakage. log_path uses parent-blob comparison for correctness (not adjacent-walk diff).

ft-identity (firetrail-c4c) shells out directly to git for user.email/user.name resolution; does NOT depend on ft-git. When ft-git's StatusReport API lands, ft-identity could be refactored to consume it (spec line 124 mentions this). Tracked as future M2/M5 polish, not required for M1.
